schema.appName: ESOP Calculator India — Stock Option Tax

faq.q: How are ESOPs taxed in India?
faq.a: ESOP taxation has two stages: (1) Exercise: FMV minus exercise price = perquisite — taxed at your income slab rate (20–30%). TDS is deducted by the employer. (2) Sale: Sale price minus FMV at exercise = capital gain — taxed as STCG (20%) if held < 1 year or LTCG (12.5% above ₹1.25 lakh) if held > 1 year.

faq.q: When is ESOP income taxed — at vesting, exercise, or sale?
faq.a: Vesting: No tax. Exercise (converting option to shares): taxed as perquisite at slab rate. Sale: taxed as capital gains. The critical tax event is exercise — this is when TDS is deducted and you must have liquidity to pay the tax or sell shares immediately.

faq.q: What is ESOP taxation for startup employees?
faq.a: Budget 2020 deferred ESOP perquisite tax for startup employees by 5 years or until sale/job change, whichever is earlier. DPIIT-recognised startups qualify. This avoids the cash crunch of paying tax on exercise without selling the shares.

examples.heading: 1,000 ESOPs: exercise at ₹100 FMV ₹800, sold at ₹1,000
Startup ESOP — 5-year tax deferral
examples.text: Same ESOP at a DPIIT startup: exercise tax deferred by 5 years. If shares are sold 3 years later at ₹2,000 each, the perquisite (₹7 lakh) is taxed when sold — by which time total gain is ₹1.9 million. Careful planning: sometimes exercising and paying tax early at lower FMV is better.
